positive_cases.name: commit-both-resources
positive_cases.trigger: write one business transaction to both PostgreSQL datasources
positive_cases.expected: both durable writes commit exactly once

positive_cases.name: schema-tools-idempotent
positive_cases.trigger: start twice from the supported schema baseline
positive_cases.expected: Flyway/Liquibase report the same reviewed schema without destructive action

failure_cases.name: bad-database-credentials
failure_cases.expected: readiness fails without leaking the secret and recovers after correction

failure_cases.name: pool-exhaustion
failure_cases.expected: bounded timeout and observable failure rather than an unbounded hang

failure_cases.name: xa-second-resource-failure
failure_cases.trigger: fail the second enlisted resource before completion
failure_cases.expected: no partial committed business state and recovery is visible

failure_cases.name: xa-restart-recovery
failure_cases.trigger: restart Liberty with an in-doubt transaction
failure_cases.expected: transaction recovery resolves or reports the exact heuristic state

failure_cases.name: schema-checksum-mismatch
failure_cases.expected: startup fails closed with no automatic repair

positive_cases.name: valid-token-and-telemetry
positive_cases.trigger: call a protected endpoint with a valid token while the telemetry backend is available
positive_cases.expected: authorized response plus correlated metric, trace, and log evidence

failure_cases.name: wrong-issuer-audience
failure_cases.trigger: use correctly signed tokens with wrong issuer and audience

failure_cases.name: jwks-outage-rotation
failure_cases.trigger: rotate signing keys while JWKS is temporarily unavailable
failure_cases.expected: cached-key policy is bounded and unknown keys fail closed

failure_cases.name: insufficient-role
failure_cases.trigger: use a valid token without the required group

failure_cases.name: dependency-readiness-down
failure_cases.trigger: make a required downstream dependency unavailable
failure_cases.expected: readiness becomes unhealthy while liveness follows the contract

failure_cases.name: telemetry-exporter-down
failure_cases.expected: application behavior follows the loss/backpressure contract without unbounded memory growth

positive_cases.name: ordered-publish-consume
positive_cases.trigger: publish keyed records through the deployed application
positive_cases.expected: payload, headers, key, partition ordering, acknowledgment, and side effects match

failure_cases.name: duplicate-delivery
failure_cases.expected: idempotent side effects with duplicate evidence

failure_cases.name: poison-record-dlq
failure_cases.expected: bounded retries and the contracted dead-letter/recovery result

failure_cases.name: broker-unavailable
failure_cases.expected: bounded failure, readiness transition, reconnect, and no silent loss

failure_cases.name: consumer-rebalance
failure_cases.trigger: scale the deployment during in-flight processing
failure_cases.expected: partition handoff preserves the acknowledgment and ordering contract

failure_cases.name: termination-drain
failure_cases.trigger: terminate a pod with in-flight messages
failure_cases.expected: graceful drain or explicitly recorded redelivery within the grace period

failure_cases.name: bad-secret-rollout
failure_cases.expected: rollout remains unready and rolls back without exposing credentials
